extra.reasoning:
This is a state machine. Property: globally (opted_out implies not sent), i.e., no reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true.

Initial state: opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0.

The defect is rule t_resume_glitch: on send_attempt, if (opted_out and ticks >= 1), it sets sent := true. This directly produces a violating state because it sets sent true while opted_out is true. When opted_out and ticks>=1, both t_send_blocked (guard opted_out) and t_resume_glitch (guard opted_out and ticks>=1) are enabled; the spec imposes no deterministic priority among multiple enabled transitions, so the t_resume_glitch execution is reachable.

Counterexample (3 events, within the 5-event bound):
1. optout: t_optout fires -> opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0.
2. tick: t_tick fires -> ticks = (0<1 ? 1 : ...) = 1, sent=false. State: opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=1.
3. send_attempt: guard (opted_out and ticks>=1) holds, t_resume_glitch fires -> sent:=true. State: opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=1.

The state after event 3 has opted_out=true and sent=true, violating (opted_out implies not sent). File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts/outreach-d1-viol-04.txt.
